hook: Popular Facebook restaurant game by Playfish that launched in 2009 and was retired in 2012 after a strong social gaming run.
fate: Retired on June 29, 2012 by Electronic Arts and Playfish. The Facebook version was taken offline and the original game became unavailable.
summary: Restaurant City was a social restaurant simulation game created by Playfish for Facebook. It entered public beta on April 28, 2009 and let players design restaurants, hire staff, collect ingredients, and visit friends. Electronic Arts acquired Playfish in November 2009 and the game was retired on June 29, 2012.
notable_products: In game furniture and decorative items
overview_md:
Restaurant City was a Facebook restaurant simulation game developed by Playfish. Players built a restaurant, arranged furniture, selected dishes, hired workers, and served customers inside a social game loop built around friends. The game stood out because restaurant design was a major part of play, not just a background system.

Players could visit friends, collect ingredients, improve recipes, and expand their restaurants over time. Progress came from a mix of decoration, menu development, staffing, and repeat daily play. That combination gave the game a strong identity during the early Facebook gaming era.

Restaurant City entered public beta on April 28, 2009. It arrived when social games on Facebook were growing quickly and helped define how management games could blend customization with friend based activity. Its appeal came from letting players treat the space as both a game system and a personal creative project.

Electronic Arts acquired Playfish in November 2009. Restaurant City continued operating under that ownership, but the game was later retired. On May 1, 2012, players were informed that Restaurant City would close on June 29, 2012.

The shutdown ended the original live version of the game and removed access to restaurants that players had built over several years. For many players, the lasting memory was not only the management loop but the way the game turned decorating, collecting, and visiting friends into one shared experience. That mix is the clearest reason Restaurant City remained memorable after its retirement.

timeline.text: Restaurant City enters public beta on Facebook through Playfish.
Game Developer coverage places Playfish among the leading social game companies during Facebook gaming growth.
Electronic Arts acquires Playfish for about 275 million dollars in cash and 25 million dollars in equity retention arrangements, with additional milestone based consideration possible.
Restaurant City players are informed that the game will be retired on June 29, 2012.
Restaurant City is retired and the original live game becomes unavailable.

seo.meta_title: What Happened to Restaurant City? Why the Facebook Game Shut Down in 2012
seo.meta_description: Restaurant City was a popular Facebook restaurant game by Playfish that launched in 2009 and shut down on June 29, 2012. Here is why it ended and what happened to the original game.
keywords: why did restaurant city shut down

faq.question: What was Restaurant City?
faq.answer: Restaurant City was a Facebook restaurant simulation game developed by Playfish. Players built and decorated restaurants, hired staff, collected ingredients, unlocked recipes, and visited friends as part of the core social gameplay.

faq.question: When did Restaurant City come out?
faq.answer: Restaurant City entered public beta on April 28, 2009 on Facebook. It became part of the early wave of major social games that grew quickly on the platform.

faq.question: Why did Restaurant City shut down?
faq.answer: Restaurant City shut down after Electronic Arts retired the game as part of broader changes to its social gaming portfolio. Players were informed in 2012 that the Facebook game would close, and the live service ended on June 29, 2012.

faq.question: When did Restaurant City shut down?
faq.answer: Restaurant City officially shut down on June 29, 2012. After that date, the original Facebook game was no longer playable.

faq.question: Can you still play Restaurant City today?
faq.answer: No. The original Restaurant City Facebook game is no longer available because the live service was retired. There is no official version of the original game still running.

faq.question: Who made Restaurant City?
faq.answer: Restaurant City was created by Playfish, a social game developer that was acquired by Electronic Arts in 2009.
